thesis: Vedanta remains a high-beta materials and demerger-unlock play with adequate (not falling) analyst revisions and slightly cheap valuation; worth a core weight but not 4.7%.
falsification: Analyst revisions roll over into the falling zone, commodity prices break down, or the demerger timeline slips materially.

thesis: Aluminium spin-out captures the metals up-cycle and demerger re-rating; held as part of a sized Vedanta complex rather than a single concentrated bet.
falsification: Demerger value-unlock thesis stalls or aluminium prices fall sharply.

thesis: Ferrous spin-out is a cyclical steel exposure with demerger optionality; sized small given no independent revision signal yet.
falsification: Steel cycle turns down or the spin-out re-rating fails to materialise.

thesis: Power spin-out rides structural India electricity demand growth; kept small pending a standalone track record.
falsification: Power demand or merchant tariffs disappoint, or the demerger value-unlock stalls.

thesis: Oil and gas spin-out adds energy exposure to the Vedanta unlock; trimmed to manage aggregate single-promoter concentration.
falsification: Energy prices fall or the demerger unlock fails to close the holding-company discount.

thesis: Gland Pharma is seeing rising analyst revisions in a stabilising US-generics/injectables cycle; an attractive risk-reward to upweight.
falsification: Revisions roll over, US injectable pricing deteriorates, or a regulatory/FDA setback hits a key facility.

thesis: P&G Health combines strong analyst revisions with a defensive consumer-health franchise — a quality core for a momentum-led book.
falsification: Revisions fade or volume/pricing growth in its core brands stalls.

thesis: Coforge carries rising analyst revisions on a strong deal pipeline; best-positioned of my IT names for a revisions-led tilt.
falsification: Revisions turn down, deal bookings slow, or discretionary IT spend weakens.

thesis: Mphasis stays as a secondary IT holding but its revision profile is less compelling, so it earns a reduced weight.
falsification: Revisions re-accelerate and BFSI-vertical demand rebounds sharply, proving the trim wrong.

thesis: Hexaware offers growth-tilted IT exposure with acceptable revisions; held smaller than Coforge as the junior IT position.
falsification: Revisions fall, margins compress, or post-listing growth disappoints.

thesis: ONGC pairs rising revisions with genuine cheapness — a rare both-signals-aligned name this round; adds large-cap energy ballast.
falsification: Crude oil falls sharply, subsidy/windfall-levy risk re-emerges, or revisions reverse.

thesis: NMDC rides firm iron-ore realisations with rising analyst revisions; a cyclical materials add that diversifies the metals book beyond Vedanta.
falsification: Iron-ore prices or steel demand weaken, or revisions roll over.

thesis: Muthoot Finance benefits from elevated gold prices lifting loan-book and yields, with rising analyst revisions; a quality NBFC add.
falsification: Gold prices fall hard, regulatory tightening on gold-loan LTVs, or revisions fade.

thesis: LIC combines rising revisions with deep value as embedded-value disclosure and VNB mix improve; a defensive financials add.
falsification: Revisions reverse, persistency/VNB margins disappoint, or the value-trap risk persists.

thesis: Equitas SFB shows rising analyst revisions as asset quality and NIMs stabilise; a higher-beta financials add for the risk-on tilt.
falsification: Microfinance/small-ticket credit costs spike or revisions turn down.

thesis: Grasim carries rising revisions on cement (via UltraTech), VSF recovery and the paints ramp; a diversified large/mid materials add.
falsification: Paints losses widen, VSF spreads compress, or revisions fade.

thesis: Great Eastern Shipping pairs rising revisions with cheapness as tanker and offshore day-rates stay firm; a cyclical cash-generative add.
falsification: Freight/charter rates fall, fleet oversupply returns, or revisions reverse.

thesis: Solar Industries rides a structural defence and mining-explosives order pipeline with rising analyst revisions; a high-quality structural add.
falsification: Defence order momentum slows, export approvals stall, or revisions roll over.

thesis: Lenskart shows rising analyst revisions on a structural organised-eyewear growth runway; a growth-tilted consumer add sized for its newness.
falsification: Path-to-profitability slips, growth decelerates, or revisions reverse post-listing.

thesis: Chambal Fertilizers pairs rising revisions with cheapness on stable subsidy economics and a good monsoon read-through; a cyclical value add.
falsification: Subsidy/gas-cost economics deteriorate, monsoon disappoints, or revisions fade.

thesis: Asian Paints shows rising analyst revisions as competitive-intensity fears moderate; a defensive consumer franchise bought as sentiment troughs.
falsification: Volume/margin pressure from new entrants intensifies or revisions reverse.

thesis: Cummins India remains a structural data-center/genset and exports beneficiary, but neutral revisions argue for a smaller weight than before.
falsification: Revisions slip into the falling zone or domestic capex/genset demand softens.

thesis: Grindwell Norton is a steady abrasives franchise but with unremarkable revisions, so it earns only a modest weight in a revisions-led book.
falsification: Industrial demand or revisions weaken further.

thesis: Nestle India is a defensive staples holding for ballast, but soft revisions cap its weight in a momentum-led book.
falsification: Revisions fall further or volume growth stalls.

thesis: HUL provides defensive large-cap staples exposure with revisions no longer falling; held as portfolio ballast, not a conviction overweight.
falsification: Rural demand or pricing power deteriorates and revisions turn down.

thesis: ZF Commercial Vehicle Systems is a CV-cycle and braking-tech play that is cheap but with cooling revisions; trimmed pending a clearer CV upturn.
falsification: CV cycle weakens or revisions fall deeper.

thesis: Bosch is a quality auto-components franchise but neutral revisions warrant a reduced weight in this round's tilt.
falsification: Auto demand softens or revisions decline further.

thesis: Schaeffler India is a structural bearings/EV-content play but with cooling revisions, so it earns a lighter weight.
falsification: Revisions keep falling or industrial/auto demand weakens.

thesis: Maruti Suzuki is the large-cap passenger-vehicle leader held for cyclical auto exposure; sized modestly given unexciting revisions.
falsification: PV demand or market-share trends weaken and revisions turn down.

thesis: Hyundai Motor India adds PV exposure but its falling revisions argue for a minimal weight versus Maruti.
falsification: Revisions deteriorate further or post-listing demand disappoints.

thesis: CRISIL is a high-quality ratings and analytics compounder, held smaller given only neutral revisions this round.
falsification: Revisions fall or ratings/research revenue growth slows.

thesis: ICRA is a second ratings-agency franchise; trimmed to avoid over-concentrating the ratings theme alongside CRISIL.
falsification: Revisions improve markedly or credit-issuance volumes surge.

thesis: Pfizer India is a defensive MNC-pharma holding consistent with the fund's MNC tilt; held at a modest weight on neutral revisions.
falsification: Revisions turn down or key-brand growth stalls.

thesis: Abbott India is a high-quality MNC consumer-health franchise that fits the mandate; held modestly given lukewarm revisions.
falsification: Revisions fall or volume growth in core brands slows.

thesis: Gillette India is a defensive MNC personal-care franchise aligned to the fund theme; held at a reduced weight on neutral revisions.
falsification: Revisions decline or grooming-category growth slows.

thesis: Sanofi India is a defensive MNC-pharma franchise that fits the fund theme; held small on neutral revisions.
falsification: Revisions turn down or core-brand growth slows.

thesis: Meesho is a structural value-commerce play held at a small weight; neutral revisions cap the size pending profitability proof.
falsification: Take-rate or path-to-profit disappoints, or revisions turn down.

thesis: MTAR Technologies shows rising revisions on a clean-energy and defence precision-components order book; a structural small/mid add.
falsification: Order execution slips, client concentration bites, or revisions reverse.

thesis: Nykaa carries rising analyst revisions as beauty BPC margins and GMV improve; a growth-tilted consumer add sized modestly.
falsification: Margin or growth disappointment, or revisions roll over.

thesis: Deepak Nitrite shows rising revisions as the chemicals destocking cycle turns; a cyclical specialty-chem add.
falsification: Chemical spreads stay weak or revisions reverse.

thesis: Poonawalla Fincorp carries rising revisions on rapid, well-capitalised loan-book growth; a higher-beta financials add for the risk-on tilt.
falsification: Credit costs rise, growth disappoints, or revisions turn down.
